'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Murakuni Masumida-jinja}} [[JA:村国真墨田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523053}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Masumida Shrine | 画像=[[File:Murakunimasumida jinja haiden.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Unuma Yamazaki-cho|Q131870129|qid=Q131870129|1=Unuma Yamazaki-cho}} 1-108 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyori|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kimpesha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|qid=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿= | 例祭=Second Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni
Masumida Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Murakuni Masumida Shrine''' (むらくにますみだじんじゃ, {{旧字体|'''村國眞墨󠄀田神󠄀社󠄁'''}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the {{ill|lt=Unuma-cho|Q11675738|qid=Q11675738|lt=Unuma area|1=Unuma}} of Kakamigahara. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kimpesha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} shrines in Kakamigahara City. === Overview === The founding period is around the 7th century. It is said that the local ruling {{ill|lt=gōzoku|Q84066|qid=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|qid=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} of this area ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigori District|Q11412369|qid=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}) founded the shrine by jointly enshrining the main deity of {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|qid=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}}, Mino Province {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, "{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}," with the main deity of Owari Province ichinomiya {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|qid=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyori|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} was also enshrined as a principal deityOn the plaque "Worship trestle of Engishiki Shikinaisha, Gifu Prefecture Kimpesha Murakuni Masumida Shrine," it is written that Murakuni no Oyori was enshrined as deity after death in praise of his achievements, and that Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto were combined afterward.. At the time of foundation, it is said to have stood about 500 meters south of the present site (along today's {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}), where a temporary shrine site now remains. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Eikyo|Q1193059|qid=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1429|Q6326|qid=Q6326|1=1429}}-{{ill|lt=1440|Q6384|qid=Q6384|1=1440}}), Ōsawa Toshiharu, a retainer of the {{ill|lt=Toki clan|Q687635|qid=Q687635|1=Toki clan}}, built {{ill|lt=Unuma Castle|Q11675737|qid=Q11675737|1=Unuma Castle}}; Murakuni Masumida Shrine became the guardian shrine of the Ōsawa family and was moved to the castle town of Unuma Castle. In {{ill|lt=1564|Q6632|qid=Q6632|1=1564}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 7), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} ordered {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|lt=Kinoshita Tokichiro|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to attack Unuma Castle and {{ill|lt=Igi Mountain Castle|Q11379405|qid=Q11379405|1=Igiyama Castle}}. Because of resistance by lord Ōsawa Jirōzaemon, the castle was difficult to capture. However, through Tokichiro's diplomacy Ōsawa yielded, and Unuma Castle was surrendered. Afterward Unuma Castle came under {{ill|lt=Ikeda Tsuneoki|Q1036482|qid=Q1036482|1=Ikeda Tsuneoki}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Inuyama Castle|Q1155484|qid=Q1155484|1=Inuyama Castle}}. After Tsuneoki's transfer, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakagawa Sadanari|1=UNKNOWN}} became lord of Inuyama Castle, and Unuma Castle remained its subordinate. In {{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|qid=Q6728|1=1584}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|Q1353633|qid=Q1353633|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}, Ikeda Tsuneoki used Unuma Castle as a base in operations against Inuyama Castle. Kawamura So-roku, who cooperated in that operation, obtained permission from {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and relocated Murakuni Masumida Shrine from the Unuma castle town to its current site. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|qid=Q2477|1=1973}} (Showa 48), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a prefectural office chief worship-designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kimpesha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-71{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=75}}. It is one of the five Kimpesha shrines in Kakamigahara. Its parishioners are the ten neighborhoods of the {{ill|lt=Unuma-cho|Q11675738|qid=Q11675738|lt=Unuma area|1=Unuma}} ({{ill|lt=Unuma Kawasaki-cho|Q131870096|qid=Q131870096|lt=Kawasaki|1=Kawasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Mitsuike-cho|Q131912299|qid=Q131912299|lt=Mitsuike|1=Mitsuike}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Nishi-cho|Q131870135|qid=Q131870135|lt=Nishi-cho|1=Nishi-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Higashi-cho|Q131870137|qid=Q131870137|lt=Higashi-cho|1=Higashi-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Minami-cho|Q131870134|qid=Q131870134|lt=Minami-cho|1=Minami-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Yamazaki-cho|Q131870129|qid=Q131870129|lt=Yamazaki|1=Yamazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Hoshakuji-cho|Q131912297|qid=Q131912297|lt=Hoshakuji|1=Hoshakuji}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Furuichiba-cho|Q131912296|qid=Q131912296|lt=Furuichiba|1=Furuichiba}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Koigi-cho|Q131912294|qid=Q131912294|lt=Koigi|1=Koigi}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Oigi-cho|Q131912298|qid=Q131912298|lt=Oigi|1=Oigi}})Plaque of Engishiki Shikinaisha Gifu Prefecture Kimpesha Murakuni Masumida Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyori|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|lt=Murakuni no Oyori no Mikoto|1=Murakuni Oyori}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} === Shrine precinct shrines === * Unuma Gokoku Shrine (spirits of the war dead of the {{ill|lt=Unuma-cho|Q11675738|qid=Q11675738|lt=Unuma area|1=Unuma}}) : Built in {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}} (Showa 21). Shrine building relocated from the {{ill|lt=Aviation Shrine|Q112336630|qid=Q112336630|1=Asahi Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Airfield|Q3275507|qid=Q3275507|1=Gifu Air Field}}. * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}) * Masumida Inari Shrine ({{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Ebisu-Daikoku Shrine (Ebisu Ōkami and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) * Mik嶽 Shrine ({{ill|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi|Q478504|qid=Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}) * Six minor shrines ** Tenman Shrine ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Akiba Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Akiba Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) ** Amanomiya Shrine ({{ill|lt=Takokami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Tak龗-no-kami|1=Kuraokami}}) ** Yamagami Shrine ({{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}) ** Suhara Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}) ** Mit鍬 Shrine ({{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}) === Memorial monuments === * Monument for soldiers in the {{ill|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|qid=Q178687|lt=Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}} * Monument for soldiers in the {{ill|lt=Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}} === Main rituals === * January 1 - New Year festival * {{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|qid=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}} - Kinen Festival and Masumida Inari Shrine festival * Last Sunday of July - Summer purification festival * August 15 - Unuma Gokoku Shrine spirit-comforting rite * Second Sunday of October - Annual festival * Mid-November - Niiname Festival * December 31 - New Year's Eve festival * First day of every month - Monthly festival === Address === * 1-108 Unuma Yamazaki-cho, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Unuma Station|Q5358768|qid=Q5358768|1=Unuma Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|qid=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} ({{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|qid=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}). * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Unuma Station|Q7497143|qid=Q7497143|1=Shin Unuma Station}} on the Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|qid=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} / {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Line|Q6810415|qid=Q6810415|1=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line}} ({{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|qid=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|qid=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni Shrine|Q11523055|qid=Q11523055|1=Murakuni Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasami Shrine|Q11398477|qid=Q11398477|1=Kasami Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11488771|qid=Q11488771|1=Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murakuni Masumida Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murakuni Masumida Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Murakuni Masumida-jinja}} [[JA:村国真墨田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523053|commons|Category:Murakuni Masumida-jinja|ja|村国真墨田神社}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Masumida Shrine | 画像=[[File:Murakunimasumida jinja haiden.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Unuma Yamazaki-cho|Q131870129|qid=Q131870129|1=Unuma Yamazaki-cho}} 1-108 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyori|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kimpesha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|qid=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿= | 例祭=Second Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni
Masumida Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Murakuni Masumida Shrine''' (むらくにますみだじんじゃ, {{旧字体|'''村國眞墨󠄀田神󠄀社󠄁'''}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the {{ill|lt=Unuma-cho|Q11675738|qid=Q11675738|lt=Unuma area|1=Unuma}} of Kakamigahara. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kimpesha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} shrines in Kakamigahara City. === Overview === The founding period is around the 7th century. It is said that the local ruling {{ill|lt=gōzoku|Q84066|qid=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|qid=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} of this area ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigori District|Q11412369|qid=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}) founded the shrine by jointly enshrining the main deity of {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|qid=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}}, Mino Province {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, "{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}," with the main deity of Owari Province ichinomiya {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|qid=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyori|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} was also enshrined as a principal deityOn the plaque "Worship trestle of Engishiki Shikinaisha, Gifu Prefecture Kimpesha Murakuni Masumida Shrine," it is written that Murakuni no Oyori was enshrined as deity after death in praise of his achievements, and that Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto were combined afterward.. At the time of foundation, it is said to have stood about 500 meters south of the present site (along today's {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}), where a temporary shrine site now remains. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Eikyo|Q1193059|qid=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1429|Q6326|qid=Q6326|1=1429}}-{{ill|lt=1440|Q6384|qid=Q6384|1=1440}}), Ōsawa Toshiharu, a retainer of the {{ill|lt=Toki clan|Q687635|qid=Q687635|1=Toki clan}}, built {{ill|lt=Unuma Castle|Q11675737|qid=Q11675737|1=Unuma Castle}}; Murakuni Masumida Shrine became the guardian shrine of the Ōsawa family and was moved to the castle town of Unuma Castle. In {{ill|lt=1564|Q6632|qid=Q6632|1=1564}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 7), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} ordered {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|lt=Kinoshita Tokichiro|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} to attack Unuma Castle and {{ill|lt=Igi Mountain Castle|Q11379405|qid=Q11379405|1=Igiyama Castle}}. Because of resistance by lord Ōsawa Jirōzaemon, the castle was difficult to capture. However, through Tokichiro's diplomacy Ōsawa yielded, and Unuma Castle was surrendered. Afterward Unuma Castle came under {{ill|lt=Ikeda Tsuneoki|Q1036482|qid=Q1036482|1=Ikeda Tsuneoki}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Inuyama Castle|Q1155484|qid=Q1155484|1=Inuyama Castle}}. After Tsuneoki's transfer, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakagawa Sadanari|1=UNKNOWN}} became lord of Inuyama Castle, and Unuma Castle remained its subordinate. In {{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|qid=Q6728|1=1584}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|Q1353633|qid=Q1353633|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}, Ikeda Tsuneoki used Unuma Castle as a base in operations against Inuyama Castle. Kawamura So-roku, who cooperated in that operation, obtained permission from {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and relocated Murakuni Masumida Shrine from the Unuma castle town to its current site. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|qid=Q2477|1=1973}} (Showa 48), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a prefectural office chief worship-designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kimpesha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-71{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=75}}. It is one of the five Kimpesha shrines in Kakamigahara. Its parishioners are the ten neighborhoods of the {{ill|lt=Unuma-cho|Q11675738|qid=Q11675738|lt=Unuma area|1=Unuma}} ({{ill|lt=Unuma Kawasaki-cho|Q131870096|qid=Q131870096|lt=Kawasaki|1=Kawasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Mitsuike-cho|Q131912299|qid=Q131912299|lt=Mitsuike|1=Mitsuike}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Nishi-cho|Q131870135|qid=Q131870135|lt=Nishi-cho|1=Nishi-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Higashi-cho|Q131870137|qid=Q131870137|lt=Higashi-cho|1=Higashi-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Minami-cho|Q131870134|qid=Q131870134|lt=Minami-cho|1=Minami-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Yamazaki-cho|Q131870129|qid=Q131870129|lt=Yamazaki|1=Yamazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Hoshakuji-cho|Q131912297|qid=Q131912297|lt=Hoshakuji|1=Hoshakuji}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Furuichiba-cho|Q131912296|qid=Q131912296|lt=Furuichiba|1=Furuichiba}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Koigi-cho|Q131912294|qid=Q131912294|lt=Koigi|1=Koigi}}, {{ill|lt=Unuma Oigi-cho|Q131912298|qid=Q131912298|lt=Oigi|1=Oigi}})Plaque of Engishiki Shikinaisha Gifu Prefecture Kimpesha Murakuni Masumida Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyori|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|lt=Murakuni no Oyori no Mikoto|1=Murakuni Oyori}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} === Shrine precinct shrines === * Unuma Gokoku Shrine (spirits of the war dead of the {{ill|lt=Unuma-cho|Q11675738|qid=Q11675738|lt=Unuma area|1=Unuma}}) : Built in {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}} (Showa 21). Shrine building relocated from the {{ill|lt=Aviation Shrine|Q112336630|qid=Q112336630|1=Asahi Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Airfield|Q3275507|qid=Q3275507|1=Gifu Air Field}}. * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}) * Masumida Inari Shrine ({{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Ebisu-Daikoku Shrine (Ebisu Ōkami and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) * Mik嶽 Shrine ({{ill|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi|Q478504|qid=Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}) * Six minor shrines ** Tenman Shrine ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Akiba Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Akiba Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) ** Amanomiya Shrine ({{ill|lt=Takokami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Tak龗-no-kami|1=Kuraokami}}) ** Yamagami Shrine ({{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}) ** Suhara Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}) ** Mit鍬 Shrine ({{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}) === Memorial monuments === * Monument for soldiers in the {{ill|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|qid=Q178687|lt=Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}} * Monument for soldiers in the {{ill|lt=Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}} === Main rituals === * January 1 - New Year festival * {{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|qid=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}} - Kinen Festival and Masumida Inari Shrine festival * Last Sunday of July - Summer purification festival * August 15 - Unuma Gokoku Shrine spirit-comforting rite * Second Sunday of October - Annual festival * Mid-November - Niiname Festival * December 31 - New Year's Eve festival * First day of every month - Monthly festival === Address === * 1-108 Unuma Yamazaki-cho, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Unuma Station|Q5358768|qid=Q5358768|1=Unuma Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|qid=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} ({{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|qid=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}). * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Unuma Station|Q7497143|qid=Q7497143|1=Shin Unuma Station}} on the Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|qid=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} / {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Line|Q6810415|qid=Q6810415|1=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line}} ({{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|qid=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|qid=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni Shrine|Q11523055|qid=Q11523055|1=Murakuni Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasami Shrine|Q11398477|qid=Q11398477|1=Kasami Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11488771|qid=Q11488771|1=Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murakuni Masumida Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murakuni Masumida Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]
